source.title: 3 VAŽNA KORAKA U HODU S BOGOM - fra Stjepan Brčina | PODCAST #109

summary.title_hr: Tri koraka u hodu s Bogom: sakramenti, srčana molitva i zajednica vjere
summary.abstract_hr: Fra Stjepan Brčina, organizator Antunovskog hoda i voditelj evangelizacijske zajednice na zagrebačkom Svetom Duhu, gostuje uoči osmog Antunovskog hoda 6. lipnja. U razgovoru s Markom Petrovićem ističe da nema duhovnosti bez sakramenata — osobito euharistije, klanjanja i ispovijedi — i pojašnjava zašto je osobna jutarnja molitva srcem nužna zaštita od duhovnih napasti. Govori i o važnosti zdrave zajednice vjere, javnog svjedočenja kršćanstva i o tome zašto je sve veći broj mladih duhovno gladan sadržaja, a ne kritike. Najavljuje franjevački kamp kao novi pastoralni projekt i aplikaciju Antunovskog hoda.
key_points: Antunovski hod nije obična šetnja nego intenzivna duhovna obnova — koncept programa od 12 sati pa do završnog euharistijskog klanjanja namjerno postupno otvara srce sudionika.
Brčina upozorava na 'online svećenike' i 'privatnu vjeru' — biti hit na internetu, a ne moći okupiti pet ljudi na misi znak je da nešto nedostaje; fizički kontakt i susret su nezamjenjivi.
Nema duhovnosti bez sakramenata: euharistija, klanjanje, ispovijed i krizma nisu opcionalni, jer Isus je rekao 'tko jede tijelo moje ima život vječni' kao izričitu zapovijed, a ne sugestiju.
Posebno argumentira važnost euharistije usporedbom sa sotonistima: oni 'na kilometre' znaju koja je hostija posvećena, što govori o stvarnoj prisutnosti koju katolici često nedovoljno cijene.
Klanjanje uspoređuje sa sunčanjem — kao što sunčanje fizički mijenja čovjeka i kad se ne osjeti odmah, tako klanjanje pred živim Bogom duhovno mijenja onoga koji u njemu sudjeluje.
Jutarnja osobna molitva srcem (a ne 'na pamet') je duhovna zaštita prije izlaska u dan; primjer Majke Terezije koja je sat vremena bila pred svetohraništem prije rada s najsiromašnijima.
Vjernik koji se srami javno svjedočiti svoju vjeru treba propitati svoj odnos s Bogom — apostoli su izlazili na trgove svjesni da mogu izgubiti život, a danas mnogi pasivno šute.
Napasti i kušnje su znak da je čovjek duhovno živ; tko nema kušnji, neka se zapita zašto, jer 'duhovno mrtve' đavo neće napadati.
Mlade ne treba kritizirati nego im davati sadržaj — Brčina najavljuje franjevački kamp kao nadogradnju postojećeg pastorala na Svetom Duhu, jer 'sadržaja nikad malo'.
summary.title_en: Three steps in the walk with God: sacraments, heartfelt prayer and community of faith
summary.abstract_en: Fra Stjepan Brčina, organizer of the Antunovski hod and leader of the evangelization community at Zagreb's Sveti Duh, is a guest ahead of the eighth Antunovski hod on June 6. In a conversation with Marko Petrović, he emphasizes that there is no spirituality without sacraments — especially the Eucharist, Eucharistic adoration, and confession — and explains why personal morning prayer of the heart is a necessary protection from spiritual temptations. He also speaks about the importance of a healthy community of faith, public witnessing of Christianity, and about why an increasing number of young people are spiritually hungry for content, not criticism. He announces a Franciscan camp as a new pastoral project and an application for the Antunovski hod.
key_points_en: Antunovski hod is not an ordinary walk but an intense spiritual renewal — the concept of the program from 12 hours until the final Eucharistic adoration intentionally gradually opens the heart of the participants.

Brčina warns against 'online priests' and 'private faith' — being a hit on the internet, and not being able to gather five people at Mass is a sign that something is missing; physical contact and encounter are irreplaceable.

There is no spirituality without sacraments: Eucharist, Eucharistic adoration, confession and confirmation are not optional, because Jesus said 'who eats my body has eternal life' as an explicit commandment, not a suggestion.

He particularly argues the importance of the Eucharist by comparison with Satanists: they 'for kilometers' know which host is consecrated, which speaks of the real presence which Catholics often insufficiently appreciate.

Eucharistic adoration compares to sunbathing — just as sunbathing physically changes a person even when it is not immediately felt, so Eucharistic adoration before the living God spiritually changes the one who participates in it.

Morning personal prayer from the heart (and not 'by rote') is spiritual protection before going out into the day; an example of Mother Teresa who was for an hour before the tabernacle before working with the poorest.

A believer who is ashamed to publicly witness their faith should question their relationship with God — apostles went out into the squares aware that they could lose their lives, while today many passively remain silent.
Temptations and trials are a sign that a person is spiritually alive; whoever has no trials, let him ask himself why, because the devil will not attack the 'spiritually dead'.
Youth should not be criticized but given content — Brčina announces a Franciscan camp as an upgrade to the existing pastoral care at Sveti Duh, because 'content is never little'.
mentioned_places_en: Basilica of Saint Anthony of Padua
